message.content: {"score":1.4,"explanation":"The speech is clearly people-centric, repeatedly emphasizing the struggles of working-class New Zealanders.","populist":{"category_1":"The gap between rich and poor widened by 127 percent.","category_2":"Between 1853 and 1984, 1,102 MPs had been elected.","category_3":"I ended up with a Labour electorate committee with 24 members, 23 unemployed.","category_4":"To beat the National Party we had to catch the political machine.","category_5":"I left the Labour Party in 1989 to form the NewLabour Party.","category_6":""},"non_populist":{"category_1":"There are now more Maori, as well as Asian and Pacific MPs.","category_2":"The gap between rich and poor widened by 127 percent in six years.","category_3":"93 percent of the population was against the sale of Telecom.","category_4":"I pay tribute to Helen Clark, to parliamentary staff.","category_5":"Kiwibank and Air New Zealand show publicly owned assets can be run successfully.","category_6":"I have been in this House, I respect the process."}}
